title: malus × floribunda var. parkmannii
intro: Malus × floribunda var. parkmannii, also known as the Parkmann's flowering crabapple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is a variant of the flowering crabapple (Malus × floribunda). It is valued for its attractive flowers and edible fruits, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to various health benefits, including reduced risk of chronic diseases and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of Malus × floribunda var. parkmannii are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and may be used to support digestive health and relieve respiratory issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
